## Aba: Sheet1

| 44828 | "Come Away Home" is GREAT but it’s not life changing like Hamilton. Hamilton made me think about my life in a new way. Hamilton lingers. This will not. |
| --- | --- |
|  | Charlie and I are ridiculous in restroom and theater and restaurant, throwing water at each other and making people crazy and I LOVE IT. |
|  | Charlie hates Kevin Smith. Swears too much. |
| 44829 | Shep threatens to stop attending football games all the time, and it always worries me. I'll miss him. I already miss him. |
|  | Shep leaves the game in the fourth quarter. I leave 10 minutes later, run like my life depends upon it, and catch up to him. Miraculously. I can't speak. |
|  | Shep watches the Hallmark Channel's Christmas movies. This man has so many facets. |
|  | Patriots lose because Mac Jones makes stupid decisions and we fumble the ball while driving for the game winning touchdown. Stupid losses most upsetting. |
| 44830 | Elysha wants to join a temple, but after growing up poor, I cannot abide a religious institution that bills its congregation. Pass a plate. Welcome all. |
|  | I take the family on a walk around the neighborhood. Walks are so good for the brain, but people never seem to do the easy things to make them feel better. |
| 44831 | Charlie and I play "Runaway" and "Come Sail Away" and sing and dance to annoy Clara. Then I play Clara's most hated song as I leave to get milk. SO FUN! |
|  | Text to Jeni: I’ve wanted a Ferris Bueller all my life, to push me like he pushed Cameron, but end up becoming a Ferris for every would-be Ferris. |
|  | Text to Jeni: Very likely related to the abandonment of my father as a boy. If you spend your entire life waiting for your father to come home, no one else ever seems good enough. |
|  | Text to Jeni: If you spend your entire life waiting for your father to come home, no one else ever seems good enough. Either that or no one keep up with me. |
|  | Text to Jeni: That, BTW, was a legit five second moment. My first via text message. Never thought of my father’s departure as abandonment, but it was. |
|  | Text to Jeni: Never realized that this is probably why I can never answer the question, “Who has been a role model for you in life?” Or “Who has been a hero to you?” |
|  | Text to Jeni: The answer: I’m still waiting for my father to be. |
| 44832 | I draw a very, very rough picture of the UK to explain dukes, earls, etc. Teresa, my UK student, is offended by my drawing. Rightfully so. |
|  | Interviewing on a podcast. Host is so nervous about talking to me. So funny. I text Elysha about it. She texts back, "I can't believe I'm married to you." |
| 44833 | Alexis doesn't want to speak at Town Meeting, but I push. I ask 19 times and she finally says yes. She crushes. Then I take a chance and ask her how it went, and she talks about being scared but now being happy. |
|  | I told Scott in today's leadership meeting that being the only one doing something is oftentimes a GOOD THING. He didn't understand that I WAS SERIOUS. |
|  | I wrote a poem to Elysha today about leaving the garage door open. I love it. I think she did, too. |
| 44834 | Baseball under the lights. The game goes late, of course, which they always seem to do once during fall ball just to hurt us. Little League doesn't understand "less is more." |
| 44835 | Scout disability class. Charlie whispers to me, "Can I tell them about Clara? Can I use her name?" So smart and savvy. |
|  | I'm at this camping trip with a Muslim mom and daughter, a little person and his son, and me and Charlie. It's like a sitcom cast. |
|  | Order of the Arrow ordeal hasn't changed. Hard labor. Bread and water. No talking. Sleeping under the stars. I'm so happy. |
|  | 600 scouts get silent on "Signs up." Still respect flag enormously. Dining hall still the same. Skits and songs still the same. Love tradition. |
|  | Elysha texts me that the photo of Charlie in the tent is cute. "He looks so happy." I need this, as I desperately want to drive home and sleep in my bed tonight. |
|  | Charlie and I set our tent up on a platform. It's like sleeping on a rock. I freeze overnight. Hurts so much. Worst night of sleeping in a long, long time. |
|  | It's not an easy night, but it's a great night. Something about occupying a small space - a tent - with someone is always special. Forgot this until now. But with my son, it's even better. |
|  | Parents can't parent. They let their kids do stupid things and make no effot to fix it. Flashlights in eyes at campfire. Skipping down ampitheater seats. |
|  | Kid with austim on leaf ID class with Hotdog is hilarious. High pitched voice constantly asking queestions and inserting info about leaves and diabetes and more. So great how much patience everyone has for him. |
|  | Charlie eats his first Pop Tart. Calls it a cracker with frosting and jelly filling. |
| 44836 | Woke up in the dark, ready to pack up at 5:30 AM, forgetting about the whole sunrise thing. Charlie and chat for an hour. |
|  | Charlie walks, scores a run, and gets the game ball. Team loses by the mercy rule. No victories yet this year. The boy is never on a winning team. |
|  | We pass by an endlessly long the drive thru at Starbucks. Elysha asks, "What the hell are these people thinking?" I agree, but the WHOLE WORLD looks like that to me. |
| 44837 | "Die, Flanders!" Charlie shouts while watching The Simpson's Treehouse of Horrors 3 with me. Also, King Kong skit features natives shouting "Mosi Tatupu! Mosi Tatupu!" |
|  | Clara does an experiment. She wants to see if you can lock the bathroom door from the outside. So she locks us out. |
|  | "Why reinvent the wheel?" I am asked. My response: "Because I believe in better wheels." No comeback. |
| 44838 | I accidentally bought cool sneakers, which is annoying, because my students and TWO ADULTS have commented on my sneakers. |
|  | Signs over sinks asking that dishes not be left in them make me so sad, because what kind of monsters need this reminder? |
|  | Ellen is Jewish and forgot that TODAY is Yom Kippur. The non Jewish spouses of Jews cannot be scolded for forgetting anymore! |
